details: Begin numerical stability analysis of rms_norm
ComputeConfigDescriptor only sets fp32_dest_acc_en=(input.dtype==float32). math_fidelity defaults to HiFi4, math_approx_mode=False, packer_l1_acc not bound. Compute config not exposed to user.
Reduce uses scaler=1/W (divide-then-sum), not 1.0 sum-then-divide. Reader emits scaler via prepare_reduce_scaler / prepare_partial_reduce_scalers (scaler stored as bfloat16).
rsqrt called with template arg fast_and_approx=false. SFPU precision still gated by APPROX compile-time macro derived from math_approx_mode (default False -> precise rsqrt).

Partial-W handled via ReducePartialScaler::last_tile_at(1) — partial scaler tile zeros out-of-bounds columns. Partial-H handled at writer (writes only valid rows); compute produces garbage in out-of-bounds rows but those are never written or accumulated row-wise.

All intermediate CBs (cb_x_sq, cb_mean_sq, cb_x_norm) use input_dtype. For bf16 input, intermediate CBs are bf16 — pack roundtrip after square loses precision. For fp32 input, intermediates are fp32 (lossless roundtrip).
No UnpackToDestMode::UnpackToDestFp32 set anywhere. For fp32 inputs, reload path goes through SrcA/SrcB which may truncate to TF32 — partial precision loss vs ideal.
